name: Ben Samuels
subject: The vanishing voices of WWII veterans
body:
The last World War II veterans are in their late 90s and 100s. They shaped the world we live in today. But their perspectives are fast disappearing.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.h21fuBvZl87OtaAa2l_oYSGldhXzv_xMgbp5NxAbPgI?) for more
---
# [The vanishing voices of WWII veterans](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=152110368&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMTEwMzY4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzMyNDIzNDAsImV4cCI6MTczNTgzNDM0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2xD8wMK0DzlzB0s4woie3isoGogQlOeV8JjyYp_eXrc)
### The last World War II veterans are in their late 90s and 100s. They shaped the world we live in today. But their perspectives are fast disappearing.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Dec 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack..p4kRcPZZx57J2cpnMB_5mlVwpbZL-eFZWg2-Yp4ijp0?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/the-lost-perspective-of-our-wwii-veterans?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

When I was in fifth grade, after a unit on U.S. history, Mr. Greco gave us an assignment: interview a World War II veteran, and report back to the class with what we'd learned.
This was in 2002. Back then, that wasn't particularly challenging: a lot of us had [grandfathers who fought in WWII](https://substack.com/redirect/c2c03845-b198-41c4-ba19-5006f5c82857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and people who didn't surely had a neighbor or a family friend who did. Most veterans were in their 70s or 80s.
Today, of course, is a different story. The youngest WWII veterans are 97.1
It feels like every few weeks, you read a new story about a "last surviving" WWII veteran: the [last of the Band of Brothers](https://substack.com/redirect/03da7782-5079-45dc-a721-e02a6827e86a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)2 or the last [Doolittle Raider](https://substack.com/redirect/00190cf2-b8fa-4d8f-aade-9d3ca71dc91b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or the last [USS ](https://substack.com/redirect/41ed28d6-c764-448d-9198-a40d17c2f566?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Arizona](https://substack.com/redirect/41ed28d6-c764-448d-9198-a40d17c2f566?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ survivor](https://substack.com/redirect/41ed28d6-c764-448d-9198-a40d17c2f566?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for instance.3 Within the next decade or so, the final World War II veteran will have died.4
**We are fast losing our tether to the people who fought to protect the world from fascism and tyranny. We 're losing the people who understand the dangers of isolationism, and why international collaboration is good.**
Understanding that maybe helps us do something about it.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTUyMTEwMzY4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzMzMjQyMzQwLCJleHAiOjE3MzU4MzQzNDAsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.FHrfeSXx6ynF1YNb0mMHyToCeBB3_rrKH8HvBol8TNg?)
### World War II veterans were ubiquitous for generations
Until recently, this wasn't an issue.
[16.4 million Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/8c7747d3-4ae8-4d58-a37d-30568d657490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) served during WWII, about 12% of the [country's population at the time](https://substack.com/redirect/459c27e2-4ccb-4270-be5f-dec07c00e487?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--an astonishingly high number.5 And that still understates the impact that WWII veterans would have for decades to come.
For generations, they were present in all walks of life--at our businesses, in our communities, and at our schools. They were also very present in American politics.
#### WWII veterans in elected office
Even against the baseline of enormous participation in World War II, veterans were disproportionately well represented in politics for decades.6
**From 1953 to 1993, every single President of the United States was a WWII veteran**.7 And at its peak in the late '60s and early '70s, [54% of U.S. Senators had served in WWII](https://substack.com/redirect/a9d21edb-f890-4ba4-84c2-db1dc22c633f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).8
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a9d21edb-f890-4ba4-84c2-db1dc22c633f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Even 40 years after that peak, there were still Senators who'd served in the War,9 building seniority and expertise and cache such that even as their number dwindled, they continued to have an outsized impact on legislating and public discourse.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=152110368&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMTEwMzY4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzMyNDIzNDAsImV4cCI6MTczNTgzNDM0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2xD8wMK0DzlzB0s4woie3isoGogQlOeV8JjyYp_eXrc)
### Why talk about this now?
The [Greatest Generation](https://substack.com/redirect/01c3abd7-4145-4e15-b0a6-d5ca5ed8db0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is valorized,10 at times in ways that sweep under the rug the challenges of wartime and post-War America. Rising prosperity in the late '40s and '50s often excluded both women and minorities, for instance, despite their contributions to the war effort.
The goal here is not to deify World War II veterans.11 Rather, it's important to acknowledge that a group of people came together to win a war whose set of goals (non-exhaustively) included:
* Defeating tyranny.
* Fighting for the rights of minorities and the discriminated against.
* Fighting for self-determination and democracy.

It's no surprise, then, that in Congress, support for NATO, for international trade, and for the rights of refugees had been strong for decades.
That's obviously quite different now. Donald Trump is threatening to [cut ties to NATO](https://substack.com/redirect/12ba544b-a5ac-403f-94c4-8f2b030489ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [blow up international trade](https://substack.com/redirect/2df39508-83da-403f-b918-af9631618c65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [block refugees from coming](https://substack.com/redirect/6e3d81b3-68e5-4c4f-8047-db29cc0e281b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the United States.
A generation ago, lawmakers would've (rightly) seen all of this as a threat to world peace and stability. They'd seen and experienced what happens when you dive head-first into isolationism.
But those people are mostly gone. **Without their collective memory, wisdom, and experience, we are squarely living in the post-post-War era.**
### Younger people don't believe in a lot of what America has stood for since 1945
[Older voters lean significantly more Republican than younger ones](https://substack.com/redirect/5e74ddf7-039f-4221-b02a-1f18bbea8276?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).12 So it's noteworthy that older people are much more aligned with a lot of what we fought for in WWII--issues that aren't in line with today's Republican Party--than younger people are.
Older people, for instance, are [more supportive of NATO](https://substack.com/redirect/b707988d-3b1b-4536-aabd-cb10d554e7ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [more supportive of Ukraine, and less supportive of Russia](https://substack.com/redirect/804040b5-596e-46d8-bcfe-e4df4aa2e81c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than younger people. Most surprisingly, perhaps, [people who are 65+ are more supportive of refugee resettlement than people under 30](https://substack.com/redirect/5dca5351-1837-4032-bee7-cfa7e5f7b412?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5dca5351-1837-4032-bee7-cfa7e5f7b412?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But all this goes even further. Younger people are _[far](https://substack.com/redirect/478b42ab-18c1-4bc8-a442-3833013685e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ likelier to see democracy as no better than a dictatorship](https://substack.com/redirect/478b42ab-18c1-4bc8-a442-3833013685e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--or worse yet, to agree that dictatorship can be good in some circumstances:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/478b42ab-18c1-4bc8-a442-3833013685e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
All of this paints an alarming picture of how younger people view the world:
1. Less supportive of democracy.
2. More supportive of isolationist policies.
3. Less supportive of refugees.

Against the framing of World War II veterans, this makes some sense. Older adults were much likelier to have grown up around--and in many cases, to have been raised by--people who fought in and lived through WWII. They may not have experienced the worst of a pre-War world themselves, but they heard it first-hand.
### As the last of the WWII veterans die, how do we protect what they fought for?
Father Time is undefeated--there's nothing we can do to fully bring these perspectives back. What was once a first-hand account is becoming a second- or a third-hand account--or people aren't learning about this at all.
Preserving the memories of people who served during World War II--and, in the years to come, the Korean War, the Civil Rights movement, the Vietnam War, etc.--is important and worthwhile.
Here's what you can do:
1. Interview the people in your life. [I recorded my grandfather talking about his service as a Marine during WWII](https://substack.com/redirect/c2c03845-b198-41c4-ba19-5006f5c82857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and have done [other similar interviews too](https://substack.com/redirect/e258e08a-4aac-433d-9518-ae5004179d74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It requires some planning, but anyone with a phone and 30 minutes can accomplish a remarkable amount.13
2. As education risks becoming more partisan and [more explicitly political](https://substack.com/redirect/e3ac2444-2013-4ba2-9e81-f09a443e5073?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), stand up for education14 that helps people understand what we were fighting for during World War II, and what we've been fighting for in the decades since.
3. In a world that's becoming increasingly inhospitable to them, support refugees by giving to organizations like [HIAS](https://substack.com/redirect/e615e6ca-61f0-4ae1-afed-e8684bb80b12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the [International Rescue Committee](https://substack.com/redirect/6f3c283d-1b20-4300-89c4-623e4d9293a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTUyMTEwMzY4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzMzMjQyMzQwLCJleHAiOjE3MzU4MzQzNDAsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.FHrfeSXx6ynF1YNb0mMHyToCeBB3_rrKH8HvBol8TNg?)
### Why I care
World War II is personal to me. I'm named after two WWII veterans, my grandfather and my great-grandfather. I had family murdered in the Holocaust.15
But even beyond that, I believe in what we fought for during WWII. I believe we have a moral obligation to help refugees; I believe we have a moral obligation to [promote peace in the world](https://substack.com/redirect/1a5a7605-061f-4130-ab5c-b150d755b557?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); I believe that multiculturalism, here and abroad, is worth defending.
We've lost most of the voices who can best, and most personally, argue for why all of that matters.
The one thing you can immediately do is talk to--and record--people in your life. Putting any politics aside: my grandfather died a few years after [I interviewed him](https://substack.com/redirect/c2c03845-b198-41c4-ba19-5006f5c82857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and I'm enormously glad now that I did.
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/14862510-23f8-4645-a9ad-a3fbb3a8eab3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)_
_I do not receive direct replies to this email. For press inquiries, please contact press@ben-samuels.com._
1
Assuming they were 18 in 1945, they were born in 1927, which means that they're 97 today. There are a handful who were younger--people lied about their age to enlist--but surely everyone is in their mid-90s or older.
2
The last Band of Brothers officer was [Edward Shames](https://substack.com/redirect/4cf06218-3230-4de9-85c9-bc7a832b2e18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [who died in 2021](https://substack.com/redirect/38c80acf-bf95-4ab0-8d71-8e02c7495086?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). His obituary included the incredible detail that at the end of the War, he stole a bottle of cognac marked "for the Fuhrer's use only" which he opened and consumed at his son's bar mitzvah. Just a top-tier example of f***-you karmic justice.
3
Since I started drafting this about a week ago, the _NYT_ wrote [another one of these articles](https://substack.com/redirect/a90c33d3-f6a4-4a13-be2b-a1fe5d571084?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is, unfortunately, happening all the time.
4
The [last World War I combat veteran](https://substack.com/redirect/d37d377f-c76a-4c88-acaa-f45bf0874799?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) died in 2011 at 110 years old. If you assume some math similar to that, the last WWII veteran will die sometime in the late 2030s. But of course, the vast majority are already gone.
After the [80th anniversary of D-Day](https://substack.com/redirect/b1dcbbab-fa83-4572-bfa6-4de1f6d967cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a few months ago, a lot of the press coverage made reference to the fact that this would likely be the final major commemoration with any survivors present. Here are [some](https://substack.com/redirect/ea9308ad-b0f6-47e9-bc22-ac26cb4efb3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [quick](https://substack.com/redirect/82e8e82c-e93d-4d27-afc1-f0e31ce7edab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [examples](https://substack.com/redirect/593b6cbe-8066-4433-92a6-ba5ff72c9964?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [of](https://substack.com/redirect/b8a0fa43-9219-40fc-8a2d-bb382c64a691?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [that](https://substack.com/redirect/8a794e0f-01d8-46e6-b3d1-e40b6bbef776?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
5
Especially when you consider that children, older people, and most women didn't serve--at least not formally in the Armed Forces.
6
Some of this has to do with the fact that positions of power were largely closed off to women. And while there were women who served in the military, of course, they did not do so at the same rate. (And often contributed to the war effort in other ways.)
7
Dwight Eisenhower, elected in 1952, became President _because_ of his service during WWII. Other Presidents had varying levels of involvement--Jimmy Carter's was very loose, for example, as he was a student at the Naval Academy towards the end of the War--but it's still a remarkable run.
8
The [data on the official U.S. Senate website](https://substack.com/redirect/a9d21edb-f890-4ba4-84c2-db1dc22c633f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is, frustratingly, not totally accurate. [Henry Bellmon](https://substack.com/redirect/b2259fcf-4903-43c3-9704-29598abfe9f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for example, did not serve from 1942-54 as the website says. (In fact, he would've been Constitutionally [ineligible to serve](https://substack.com/redirect/56d62437-e742-4949-a760-0aff9ea6f0c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for part of that window, as he wasn't 30 yet.) He instead served from 1969-81\. I corrected this specific error but otherwise did the best that I could without going line-by-line to fact-check this data. (This was necessary to preserve my sanity.) Ahh, the joys of government data.
9
Wisconsin State Senator [Fred Risser](https://substack.com/redirect/9704c965-44cb-4007-92f2-112f1523afdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who retired in 2021, was the last elected official in the U.S. to have served in WWII. Risser had an astonishingly long career in elected office: he was in the Wisconsin State Assembly for six years starting in 1957, and then he served in the Wisconsin State Senate from 1962 through 2021.
10
I mean, that generation's name is literally "the Greatest Generation," which is pretty good evidence of said valorization.
11
There were plenty of remarkable people who served during WWII, and plenty of bad ones too. [Albert Sabin](https://substack.com/redirect/99ab5162-4a3b-4305-ab96-837eb12064cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Cesar Chavez](https://substack.com/redirect/eb65edb4-eb27-46ad-8412-d60e501e20cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) were WWII veterans; so were [George Wallace](https://substack.com/redirect/05c398b4-2a38-407f-9bfd-03d2703be9df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [L. Ron Hubbard](https://substack.com/redirect/8faa8d5b-2b8b-4929-904f-a8cfe9230a89?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (L. Ron Hubbard may have taken that deification a bit too literally.)
12
Younger voters [moved to the right](https://substack.com/redirect/9ca1c97a-ceb1-4af4-a349-febae11fb132?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in this most recent election--[especially younger men](https://substack.com/redirect/a1ba9d69-c84a-4e57-955e-7bd0b26ac9a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But they're still meaningfully to the left of older voters.
13
As you can tell from the interview that I did, it's hardly professional-grade work. And that's just fine.
14
What does this mean, concretely? A lot of it is critical but isn't glamorous: vote; volunteer at schools; run for school board; call your local and state representatives (who have much more say on education than anyone federally); be aware of what's happening at schools in your area. In St. Louis, a lot of the [most troubling school board candidates](https://substack.com/redirect/41d9373c-a018-40b1-b4e3-2b24d4b0329e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) lost back in April, so there is positive momentum here--but the [elections had turnouts below 20%](https://substack.com/redirect/79ef5bd4-8ff5-452d-bde7-54646a447556?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), meaning that any individual's involvement goes a lot further.
15
In my extended family, those ties run even deeper--like they do for a lot of Americans. I had a cousin who was killed in Normandy three weeks after D-Day, and a cousin who died, decades later, with the [crippling effects of polio he contracted during the War](https://substack.com/redirect/d3b4586e-316c-4d48-b4d7-c38d4bcbcd0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

---

---

They shaped the world we live in today. But their perspectives are fast disappearing. | | | Dec 3 | | | | | | When I was in fifth grade, after a unit on U.S. history, Mr. Greco gave us an assignment: interview a World War II veteran, and report back to the class with what we'd learned. This was in 2002. Back then, that wasn't particularly challenging: a lot of us had , and people who didn't surely had a neighbor or a family friend who did. Most veterans were in their 70s or 80s. Today, of course, is a different story. The youngest WWII veterans are 97.1 It feels like every few weeks, you read a new story about a "last surviving" WWII veteran: the or the last or the last , for instance.3 Within the next decade or so, the final World War II veteran will have died.4 We are fast losing our tether to the people who fought to protect the world from fascism and tyranny. We 're losing the people who understand the dangers of isolationism, and why international collaboration is good. Understanding that maybe helps us do something about it. ? World War II veterans were ubiquitous for generations Until recently, this wasn't an issue. served during WWII, about 12% of the astonishingly high number.5 And that still understates the impact that WWII veterans would have for decades to come. For generations, they were present in all walks of life--at our businesses, in our communities, and at our schools. They were also very present in American politics. WWII veterans in elected office Even against the baseline of enormous participation in World War II, veterans were disproportionately well represented in politics for decades.6 From 1953 to 1993, every single President of the United States was a WWII veteran.7 And at its peak in the late '60s and early '70s, | | ---|---|--- Even 40 years after that peak, there were still Senators who'd served in the War,9 building seniority and expertise and cache such that even as their number dwindled, they continued to have an outsized impact on legislating and public discourse. Why talk about this now? The is valorized,10 at times in ways that sweep under the rug the challenges of wartime and post-War America. Rising prosperity in the late '40s and '50s often excluded both women and minorities, for instance, despite their contributions to the war effort. The goal here is not to deify World War II veterans.11 Rather, it's important to acknowledge that a group of people came together to win a war whose set of goals (non-exhaustively) included: Defeating tyranny. Fighting for the rights of minorities and the discriminated against. Fighting for self-determination and democracy. It's no surprise, then, that in Congress, support for NATO, for international trade, and for the rights of refugees had been strong for decades. That's obviously quite different now. Donald Trump is threatening to , , and to the United States. A generation ago, lawmakers would've (rightly) seen all of this as a threat to world peace and stability. They'd seen and experienced what happens when you dive head-first into isolationism. But those people are mostly gone. Without their collective memory, wisdom, and experience, we are squarely living in the post-post-War era. Younger people don't believe in a lot of what America has stood for since 1945 So it's noteworthy that older people are much more aligned with a lot of what we fought for in WWII--issues that aren't in line with today's Republican Party--than younger people are. Older people, for instance, are , than younger people. Most surprisingly, perhaps, : | | ---|---|--- But all this goes even further. Younger people are worse yet, to agree that dictatorship can be good in some circumstances: | | ---|---|--- All of this paints an alarming picture of how younger people view the world: 1. Less supportive of democracy. 2. More supportive of isolationist policies. 3. Less supportive of refugees. Against the framing of World War II veterans, this makes some sense. Older adults were much likelier to have grown up around--and in many cases, to have been raised by--people who fought in and lived through WWII. They may not have experienced the worst of a pre-War world themselves, but they heard it first-hand. As the last of the WWII veterans die, how do we protect what they fought for? Father Time is undefeated--there's nothing we can do to fully bring these perspectives back. What was once a first-hand account is becoming a second- or a third-hand account--or people aren't learning about this at all. Preserving the memories of people who served during World War II--and, in the years to come, the Korean War, the Civil Rights movement, the Vietnam War, etc.--is important and worthwhile. Here's what you can do: 1. Interview the people in your life. , and have done . It requires some planning, but anyone with a phone and 30 minutes can accomplish a remarkable amount.13 2. As education risks becoming more partisan and , stand up for education14 that helps people understand what we were fighting for during World War II, and what we've been fighting for in the decades since. 3. In a world that's becoming increasingly inhospitable to them, support refugees by giving to organizations like and the . ? Why I care World War II is personal to me. I'm named after two WWII veterans, my grandfather and my great-grandfather. I had family murdered in the Holocaust.15 But even beyond that, I believe in what we fought for during WWII. I believe we have a moral obligation to help refugees; I believe we have a moral obligation to ; I believe that multiculturalism, here and abroad, is worth defending. We've lost most of the voices who can best, and most personally, argue for why all of that matters. The one thing you can immediately do is talk to--and record--people in your life. | | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcmViZWthaGpvbmVzL3AvaHVycmljYW5lLWNsaW1hdG9sb2d5LWRhdGE_.u_8rkg4yHNHHngVhCLrTErNkWHsMES9nNFx0MbYiEQ8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/rebekahjones/p/hurricane-climatology-data?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I'm often asked what kind of data hurricane climatologists work with. As I was writing up a kind of overview, I realized I needed to explain the basics as to why we use this data, where our data is less-than-ideal, and how science is advancing our records.
## What global oceanic systems impact hurricane development?
Hurricanes are heat engines, drawing their power from both the warm ocean waters and the instability of the atmosphere.
There are three primary oceanic oscillations that have a major impact on hurricane development - El Nino Southern Oscillation **(ENSO),** Atlantic Multidecadal Oscillation **(AMO),** and the Pacific Decadal Oscillation **(PDO).**
Each of these operates at different timescales and has different variables impacting its relative strength.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a26f03f6-b97d-4092-9497-4af1cceb8508?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When you run analysis on hurricane frequency and intensity along with all three oscillations, patterns emerge that demonstrate the influence of each of these systems.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e56cbe7f-1606-42b8-ba86-8c8eb73abde0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The average number of named tropical cyclones, hurricanes and major hurricanes in the North Atlantic basin during various ENSO, AMO and PDO phases and combinations, 1870-2024.
From the above chart, you can see that combination of events that produce the lowest average number of storms (ENSO+/AMO-/PDO-) has less than half the average activity as the combination with the most storms (ENSO-/AMO+/PDO-)...

Hi team —
In keeping with the spirit of the season, I want to let you know just how grateful I am to have you with us in this movement for our future.
**On behalf of the entire NextGen team, thank you for all your support this year.**
[This Giving Tuesday, we’re highlighting the progress we’ve made and the work that still needs to be done. I know that none of us were expecting the outcome of this year’s election. And if I’m being honest, there are a lot of reasons to be afraid of what comes next.
_But we have to remember and celebrate the wins that**did** come our way._
Thanks to young voters, we were able to defeat the right-wing extremist running for governor in North Carolina.
Young Arizona voters fought to enshrine abortion rights in their state’s constitution and won.
Pennsylvania voters kept the far-right from taking over their state legislature.
We were even able to make sure congressional rockstars like Jasmine Crockett in Texas and Rashida Tlaib in Michigan could keep their seats and continue fighting against the MAGA agenda.
**We may not have won everything, but what we did win is critical in this fight for our future.**
_**So what happens now?**_
We keep fighting for our rights and urging young voters to reject Project 2025 and Trump’s dangerous agenda.
We hold our elected officials accountable by demanding they take action against extremism.
We keep up our work educating and empowering vulnerable communities, even when all hope seems lost.
[We’re already looking ahead in this fight for our future. And if we’re going to succeed, we’re going to need the continued support of this incredible network of grassroots supporters. clean_body: | Hi team — In keeping with the spirit of the season, I want to let you know just how grateful I am to have you with us in this movement for our future. On behalf of the entire NextGen team, thank you for all your support this year. | I know that none of us were expecting the outcome of this year’s election. And if I’m being honest, there are a lot of reasons to be afraid of what comes next. But we have to remember and celebrate the wins thatdid come our way. Thanks to young voters, we were able to defeat the right-wing extremist running for governor in North Carolina. Young Arizona voters fought to enshrine abortion rights in their state’s constitution and won. Pennsylvania voters kept the far-right from taking over their state legislature. We were even able to make sure congressional rockstars like Jasmine Crockett in Texas and Rashida Tlaib in Michigan could keep their seats and continue fighting against the MAGA agenda. We may not have won everything, but what we did win is critical in this fight for our future. So what happens now? We keep fighting for our rights and urging young voters to reject Project 2025 and Trump’s dangerous agenda. We hold our elected officials accountable by demanding they take action against extremism. We keep up our work educating and empowering vulnerable communities, even when all hope seems lost. | | | | | | In solidarity, Tzintzún Ramirez President & Executive Director NextGen PAC | Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; ; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | = |

* * *
Many of us with disabilities know firsthand how vital the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) is for ensuring that we can access basic nutrition.
Shockingly, Republicans in Congress are contemplating slashing $30 billion from SNAP to _fund tax cuts for their wealthy donors_. This heartless move threatens to increase hunger and deepen food insecurity at a time when many are already struggling to put food on the table.
**Despite the high cost of groceries being a central concern for voters, MAGA Republicans are pushing to gut this highly effective hunger-reducing program.**
**[Stand with us to tell Congress NO cuts to SNAP. Sign our petition today to protect the most vulnerable members of our society and ensure that families aren't left in the cold.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38zUwJ9QUdfy6Cxl8IJ8U7w3Np8VYtjjD_aUydVGvSzTllBnUeGSY57jhgxgRQLQJhpp3_6ZKTCf948ngGTmBReXSPETioGtLOrnNwu83PiU_MTbl1HeFBjuc28CfBYkLxk6jh-0x5HKlUKNXEMv_2RgorHtTbCVQbCyWlEHgXa6FIDl_ociMM6TTOmr5FW_AcV6KG3wVNXpFP2c7pNLZpu4G1cEP8ThP2HhbZNPlUlDPMD6DGgeKDZXXdomyH-c0evVw46ZaYl8byYepp06deeWvR_jPIwtCv-rl1EgKjs0ah6Kx8rKayaCpiO55QmZGTzcHUP4Eud_GcHV61q-v5j_HtYptkuLTlQVxi9q4d149nvMZrW2Gs-HpIy7rvN-reYDRGF-g80zf4kA4-aN1OliczABpwIQdzYRDAUSGvLoS_01qQXsBMNb_4J3sIfyHXD-kcNYYI861DJGR5qFjo5Ihi9uJD-OrrWC9kn6drFLVb-NGISi66pprrGhLBzjBI9qVIM0Lf1jhklBnAWaR6N/4bz/DTkFi8s0SvOlr3-WRuw6Yg/h2/h001.r2g0JRjAIAdJ2d2zJ9fyqwicB6-RhFo9VUkhNvTsUrM)

It's important to note that 86 percent of SNAP benefits go to households that include a child, elderly person, or person with disabilities. Moreover, about 92 percent of benefits support households living at or below the federal poverty line.

Cutting SNAP isn't just cruel; it's economically shortsighted. Every $5 spent through SNAP generates roughly $9 in economic activity.

**This isn't just a policy choice; it's a blatant prioritization of the wealthy over the welfare of everyday Americans. Recent polling shows that 85 percent of Americans want SNAP benefits increased, not cut!**

**[We need to unite and FIGHT to protect SNAP benefits! Join us and tell Congress NO cuts! The time to act is NOW!](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38zUwJ9QUdfy6Cxl8IJ8U7w3Np8VYtjjD_aUydVGvSzTllBnUeGSY57jhgxgRQLQJhpp3_6ZKTCf948ngGTmBReXSPETioGtLOrnNwu83PiU_MTbl1HeFBjuc28CfBYkLxk6jh-0x5HKlUKNXEMv_2RgorHtTbCVQbCyWlEHgXa6FIDl_ociMM6TTOmr5FW_AcV6KG3wVNXpFP2c7pNLZpu4G1cEP8ThP2HhbZNPlUlDPMD6DGgeKDZXXdomyH-c0evVw46ZaYl8byYepp06deeWvR_jPIwtCv-rl1EgKjs0ah6Kx8rKayaCpiO55QmZGSlTrSkg7h7E2RuDTwY8HJneBP9e5Lhhgj9TyGVJIBoEj2Nawdb2a4zwIKPF018HQJvMmvRUyv1WMXED3JL78SFmoqYhjG0RdL1SVx6ne4Qd7ql8s_SU3Fo3ylCaS5gYnVLnnGYWFMWZbFKmJPoo3y-Xk0ltXgVuI1y9L4AnIUdUNx5xUV5FLSl7DU4bywiEMsUyFrizAiXTZruNHyh6n1J/4bz/DTkFi8s0SvOlr3-WRuw6Yg/h4/h001.ukXsJ5QXPJkfC26oyi6uNsjXmKnqKudDWvYWHhNrKxo)**

| | Friend, Today is Giving Tuesday, a day when Americans unite to support causes that make a difference. At Club for Growth, we’re on the front lines fighting to shrink the size and scope of government, unleash pro-growth policies, and hold every legislator accountable on policy—no matter their party. name: Lena Moffitt, Evergreen
subject: The final push: We won't let Big Oil win!
disclaimer_text: paid for by Evergreen Action
body:
We're almost there—your gift will get us over the finish line
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3100766/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Friend,

Right now, in courtrooms and congressional chambers, the fight for our climate future is happening—and we cannot afford to sit this one out.

**Your donation today will be TRIPLED, helping us fight back harder than ever and make sure that these corporate polluters don't win.**[**Will you help power this fight?**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3100767/87SdQ4wZ) |
---
[TRIPLE YOUR IMPACT THIS GIVING TUESDAY](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3100768/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| We're facing an uphill battle against powerful corporate polluters determined to derail progress, but Evergreen Action is ready to meet the moment with bold solutions and unrelenting advocacy. Our strategy is clear: |
---
|
* **Halt pending fossil fuel projects before inauguration day.** We're targeting six super-polluting liquefied natural gas projects that Biden has the power to stop right now.
* **Supercharge state-level leadership** with strategic policy roadmaps and strong advocacy rooted in local partnerships and coalitions.
* **Defend the Inflation Reduction Act** from relentless attacks by Big Oil and their cronies in Congress.
* **Strengthen coalitions** that amplify the voices of frontline communities, environmental advocates, and workers demanding real, systemic change.

|
---
| ⏱️ With just 48 days on the clock before Biden is out of office, we're working diligently to prepare our allies with the tools, strategies, and support they need to defend the progress we've made and push for more.

**But we can't do it without you.**

Every dollar you give helps us stand with communities on the frontlines of climate disasters, empower advocates to fight back, and ensure that Congress doesn't roll back historic progress. We won't give up. We know that our planet and our communities are worth it. So we're going to keep fighting—not just for today, but for the decades to come.

Big Oil is counting on us to back down. Let’s show them the strength of a movement united.

[**Chip in now and have your gift TRIPLED before midnight on Giving Tuesday.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3100769/87SdQ4wZ)

Together, we'll protect the progress we've fought for and demand the bold action our future deserves. |
---
[DONATE NOW](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3100770/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| In solidarity, |
---
| | |
---
### Lena Moffitt
Executive Director
Evergreen Action

* The National Day of Mourning and what it means today.

name: MarkKelly.com
subject: Mark Kelly is personally requesting your endorsement
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
If you’re reading this email, it’s because you had a role in getting Mark Kelly to this point.
#

Team —
If you’re reading this email, it’s because you had a role in getting Mark Kelly to this point.
Maybe you’ve been with him since his first campaign for Senate.
Maybe you came in to help him win a full term.
Maybe you’ve been backing him up as he fought to help democrats up and down the ballot over the last two years.
No matter when you joined us or why you did, we’re incredibly grateful you’re here. And Mark is incredibly grateful that you are on this team.
And as we push forward and prepare for the challenges ahead, one thing is certain: Mark is going to need a strong team behind him as we face down four more years of Donald Trump.
So today, we’re respectfully asking you to sign on and personally endorse Mark Kelly as he serves Arizonans in the Senate fighting for what’s right.
[**Will you add your name to say that Mark Kelly has your support? It’s a small act, but when you add your name alongside countless others it speaks volumes about what we’re trying to accomplish together.**](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/endorse_mark_kelly_dec24_/?source=em241203-1-120&refcode2=5932.6688874.fabFJ8&akid=5932%2E6688874%2EfabFJ8)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.markkelly.com/signup/endorse_mark_kelly_dec24_/?source=em241203-1-120&refcode2=5932.6688874.fabFJ8&akid=5932%2E6688874%2EfabFJ8)**
We know that Mark would be honored if you renewed your support as we prepare for whatever comes next.
Thank you in advance,
Team Kelly

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=5932%2E6688874%2EfabFJ8).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

body:
Writing the next chapter |
---

In honor of Giving Tuesday, we're launching a three-day **Grassroots Giving Fund** to invest in the EMILYs List women who are changing the face of power and writing the next chapter in American politics.

Show your thanks to our fearless pro-choice champions and donate toward our $75,000 Grassroots Giving Fund goal today: | [DONATE NOW](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98083020/515501872/162262288?refcode=WTEFEX03_20241203_ItsGiving_PL_v1s3&sourceid=1055468&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmM4NzAzOS05N2IxLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R1_hVrVgh7AIPeG7-saNmnpFCOv9yK3A-hiSp9pIG6o=)

Democratic pro-choice women face a number of obstacles when running for office, _especially_ when they're up against deep-pocketed, extremist opponents or anti-choice billionaire backers. But we at EMILYs List help break down those obstacles — providing our candidates with the resources and support they need to run winning campaigns.

**EMILYs List is a game-changer for women running for office. But this work is only possible because of you.**

Running for office as a Democratic pro-choice woman takes strength, and we want to show our support for the women who are standing up to anti-choice extremism, fighting for our rights, and protecting our democracy.

body:
I was the only elected Republican peacefully protesting on January 6th.

| | |

| |

| | Here’s a side-by-side of my mugshot with President Trump’s.
---
[](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=94424e7812a5409f9835b114823ba547&_e=bunyWgP4Xi5w3ezYFuhPXv-LrSiilksfA2mDW6y2jRmgX4y0inoF-2A3778bch1G79fO2ubE7zaRLDef0PBeRXy8fb1VH5Ln7JYq1zp0nzZhMEutSG1TrIrbzrScBTiFO0biIaHQlhP4WVC1d_1zwuUqycTnj_FLAn89p07UGLSXjGPIMPzroqCcHOAJgHHIEBlyQnznMAOiwUdq4-9lZOZu1PUuPc0dlaU7LOY9qcaWlnY1NlFyHZi_yS-4nh2Ra0BTV8LocEC3nSgaN7eIf_0AeoZssjoTpMtEkxlXbKVSQtibWdV_4Ufshs-rVI9OkXflBabUbrsgsVXa_QBryzRlrJxnRArZ7pTLSZ__qCb6nK2ad1lC98JaI_1Ig1SU)
---
I was the only elected Republican peacefully protesting on January 6th. And the Left tried to make an example of me for it.Locking me away in solitary confinement for 90 days to try to mentally break me.But they could never break me – just like you – I’ll never stop fighting for our President.Friend, you and I stood by him when things were tough. Unlike cowardly establishment “Republicans” who backstabbed him to stay in power.And now I need you to help me hold them accountable.It starts with “Republican” Senator Shelley Moore Capito.Here’s what she said about January 6th:
---
_“It was horrifying, It was unfathomable they would come into the Chamber and defile the building. I think it was the lowest day”. “I think the president does own this,”_ she added
---
Shelley Moore Capito betrayed our president, betrayed the Americans that elected her, and continues to try to undermine President Trump’s new administration. With razor thin margins in the senate, we absolutely cannot afford to have a RINO never-Trumper in the senate undermining this administration at every turn.It’s time for her reckoning.It’s time to stomp out the snakes. **[Please rush a contribution to my campaign to Defeat Shelley Moore Capito today](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=94424e7812a5409f9835b114823ba547&_e=bunyWgP4Xi5w3ezYFuhPXv-LrSiilksfA2mDW6y2jRmgX4y0inoF-2A3778bch1G79fO2ubE7zaRLDef0PBeRXy8fb1VH5Ln7JYq1zp0nzZhMEutSG1TrIrbzrScBTiFO0biIaHQlhP4WVC1d_1zwuUqycTnj_FLAn89p07UGLSXjGPIMPzroqCcHOAJgHHIEBlyQnznMAOiwUdq4-9lZOZu1PUuPc0dlaU7LOY9qcbeJsRjlLwoHX7mVJrPoii1Jz58kHcvxYLeZ3pqrvZocrbTgI8OIhzcOogY3oRAzbzvmKmds4-NkM_ioeJe2IaShdD1B426chZOC1cIJc34dMDEHUbhK6v5SXo8Y9an-BfWxZKg29Fe5Brxxyje0M7Z)**
---
[ **DONATE **](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=94424e7812a5409f9835b114823ba547&_e=bunyWgP4Xi5w3ezYFuhPXv-LrSiilksfA2mDW6y2jRmgX4y0inoF-2A3778bch1G79fO2ubE7zaRLDef0PBeRXy8fb1VH5Ln7JYq1zp0nzZhMEutSG1TrIrbzrScBTiFO0biIaHQlhP4WVC1d_1zwuUqycTnj_FLAn89p07UGLSXjGPIMPzroqCcHOAJgHHIEBlyQnznMAOiwUdq4-9lZOZu1PUuPc0dlaU7LOY9qcbDYMb32reRuM2j6fCk1-OIgo4f6eymjm9MCnw5O7RelXr_C6mNELtJrVuoz6Co5uSTna6K9tOpOBywRfmbnt2hJrAcBMXbNznhPzeV9B_N2B-8cxDbXZps1MbH0oT5izWXGWwKU8Wz2AAn1TOedUIc)

name: Left Action
subject: Tell President Biden to secure as much climate action as possible, while he still can
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell President Biden to take immediate action on climate](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5cb1d09c-c5eb-49cb-af2f-b8efc058b004/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1b22b992-3915-4370-bb22-8a7919b82b23/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | We know the impact of climate change is here — but the next administration will do little to help, or will make things worse. President Biden needs to take immediate and urgent action on environmental safeguards wherever possible, and ensure that they’re built to last. Big Oil and Gas Companies are drilling like crazy across the US to produce gas for plastics factories or to ship overseas. We can’t keep doing this, for another 4 years — or 40. We all need to act. But the President should do everything possible in his remaining time as a global leader. The President can declare a climate emergency, reinstate a crude oil export ban, and promote rapid clean energy development. The President can support strong methane rules, and set states up to help them implement faster. [**Add your voice now: Tell President Biden to take immediate, urgent action on climate — before it’s too late.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2ecb7212-a888-4e82-a146-94a7dff9216f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T74a361ba-66a9-4a71-80a4-29f1378d2178/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Thank you, Left Action
---
|
---

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: By the numbers…
body:
---
Derek, As we begin the final month of 2024, we want to take a minute to look back and share a few numbers with you: **$38.37.** That was the DNC’s average online gift during this election cycle. Here is how we put those contributions to work: Thanks to your support, the DNC was able to invest in the Democratic parties of all **57** states and territories. That translated into critical down-ballot wins, not just in battleground states, but in states like Arkansas, where the DNC’s investments helped Democrat Diana Gonzales Worthen flip a Republican-held state House seat by **472** votes. This was Arkansas Democrats’ first net gain of legislative seats in 20 years -- and they credit the DNC’s State Party Innovation Fund grant as key to this victory. Wins like these are only possible by investing in every state, in as many races as possible, up and down the ballot -- and that’s the strategy Democrats will build on as we look ahead to 2025, 2026, and beyond. But to do that, we need to make sure we don’t lose the infrastructure we’ve built across the country. We know you might be tired or not ready to engage, and that’s okay. [**But if you’re ready to help us keep building the strong Democratic Party we’ll need as we look to the fights ahead, we hope you’ll consider matching our $38.37 average gift today:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2024?amount=38.37&refcode=em_20241203_2358_fr&refcode2=2361_22930333_XdYOZU&akid=2361%2E22930333%2EXdYOZU) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $38.37](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2024?express_lane=true&amount=38.37&refcode=em_20241203_2358_fr&refcode2=2361_22930333_XdYOZU&akid=2361%2E22930333%2EXdYOZU)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2024?refcode=em_20241203_2358_fr&refcode2=2361_22930333_XdYOZU&akid=2361%2E22930333%2EXdYOZU)**
---
****
Thank you, and more soon.
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Trump victory signals a cultural shift
body:
Guy Ciarrocchi | B+L Featured Columnist | | | |
---
| | |
---
| **__Guy Ciarrocchi:_ Trump’s historic victory reveals as much about us as about him_**
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=818b62737f&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
* * *
---
| “Trump’s victory was historic — meaning it’s important and worth studying. The unfortunate reality is that in today’s America, any Republican win in a swing state or district is actually historic. However, Trump’s victory is in a “super-historic” category.

With all the post-election debate over why he won, too many have failed to take note of how much adversity he had to overcome in order to win — and not the obvious adversity. Perhaps more importantly, his victory says a lot about the mindset of an emerging majority in our nation. What most of us are looking to find, looking to stop, or looking to fix.

The win underscores how academia, the legacy media, social media and Hollywood are, in fact, disconnected from our lives, our reality, and our values.

When we consider what he overcame to win, it says a great deal about the state of the nation..."
---
| [Read More](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=63d048cc38&e=b7b3eddce2 "Read More")
---
|
---
|
## About Guy Ciarrocchi:
---
| | [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9f472936a3&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
As a common-sense conservative thought-leader, Guy’s writings provoke thought on important topics such as education, politics, culture and anti-Semitism. Guy draws on decades of experience from South Philly to the Philadelphia suburbs to Pittsburgh, from Harrisburg to Washington, DC, having served at the highest levels of government, as a candidate and strategist, as well as Chamber CEO. Guy has been listening, observing and guiding his readers for years.
---
|

clean_body: Guy Ciarrocchi | B+L Featured Columnist | | | | | | | | Guy Ciarrocchi: Trump’s historic victory reveals as much about us as about him | | | “Trump’s victory was historic — meaning it’s important and worth studying. The unfortunate reality is that in today’s America, any Republican win in a swing state or district is actually historic. However, Trump’s victory is in a “super-historic” category. With all the post-election debate over why he won, too many have failed to take note of how much adversity he had to overcome in order to win — and not the obvious adversity. Perhaps more importantly, his victory says a lot about the mindset of an emerging majority in our nation. What most of us are looking to find, looking to stop, or looking to fix. The win underscores how academia, the legacy media, social media and Hollywood are, in fact, disconnected from our lives, our reality, and our values. When we consider what he overcame to win, it says a great deal about the state of the nation..." | "Read More" | | About Guy Ciarrocchi: | | As a common-sense conservative thought-leader, Guy’s writings provoke thought on important topics such as education, politics, culture and anti-Semitism. Guy draws on decades of experience from South Philly to the Philadelphia suburbs to Pittsburgh, from Harrisburg to Washington, DC, having served at the highest levels of government, as a candidate and strategist, as well as Chamber CEO. Guy has been listening, observing and guiding his readers for years. | | We at are proud to present Guy's writings, and to provide readers across our region, state and beyond, with insightful, original investigative reports, and thought-leadership, on education, crime, business and government. Peter, we’ll keep this brief:
1. While the final results of this November’s election were tough, we still have an opportunity to elect a wave of down-ballot Georgians in the runoff elections.

2. And there are 18 key local level seats from state House and Senate to city council that will help shape the agenda for communities across the Peach State.

3. But the polls don’t close until 7PM, so we still have time to boost voter turnout!

That’s why we need to hit our final $10,000 fundraising goal so that we can reach out to voters and get them to the polls in these final hours.
**But we’re almost out of time, so we need your help.[ Will you please chip in $15 right now to the Democratic Party of Georgia??](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98086652/515514060/-871140472?amount=15&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_120324_1_3_Runoff-Election&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjY3MDcyMi05YWIxLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EMRryPsFnK5UhQUtH1By4sX8EL6VpYA0A8wAXRrEflw=)**

name: Ro Khanna
subject: It's time to abolish super PACs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]

Friend,

Are you sick of billionaire-dominated elections? Me too. Super PACs and other independent expenditures spent over $4.4 billion in this year’s federal elections, with over 150 billionaire families contributing more than $1.9 billion.

It doesn’t have to be that way.

In a new Boston Globe op-ed [https://www.bostonglobe.com/2024/12/01/opinion/citizens-united-superpacs-restricting-contributions/] , Harvard Law professor Lawrence Lessig and I break down how super PACs encourage quid pro quo corruption and how we can break their hold over our democracy.

https://www.bostonglobe.com/2024/12/01/opinion/citizens-united-superpacs-restricting-contributions/ [https://www.bostonglobe.com/2024/12/01/opinion/citizens-united-superpacs-restricting-contributions/]

I’ve never taken PAC or lobbyist money. When I was first elected, I co-founded the No PAC Caucus and introduced a bill to ban all PAC and lobbyist money from Congress, because I firmly believe that our government should work for the people—NOT special interests.

Please take a few minutes to read our op-ed [https://www.bostonglobe.com/2024/12/01/opinion/citizens-united-superpacs-restricting-contributions/] on how we can abolish super PACs. Text me at (650) 999-9610 to let me know your thoughts.

Thank you for your activism and all that you do.

In solidarity,
Ro

Wait! One more thing–

Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.

If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

DONATE $3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20241203-bogo-super-pacs-oped&amount=3]
DONATE MORE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20241203-bogo-super-pacs-oped]

https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com] https://www.facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA [https://www.facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA] https://twitter.com/RoKhanna [https://twitter.com/RoKhanna] info@rokhanna.com [info@rokhanna.com]

Ro for Congress
P.O Box 3513
Santa Clara, CA 95051
United States

Paid for by Ro for Congress

Dear Friend,
---
Around the world today, non-profits are raising money for the specific charities they represent for Giving Tuesday. Let Us Worship is a Kingdom-First ministry, and we seek to see revival sweep across America and the world. We see Giving Tuesday as _Kingdom Tuesday_!
---
[**CONTRIBUTE TO GIVING TUESDAY >>> **](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=249db20cce094cd696c27768ed9b63ad&_e=UObmW_65_LniOMe-wwf6BpsUvvmc3B9lsQuPHadzZ9h5WTTVonagF4Vt459uGs6DwiT96N6-Xgpm5l5IVGUxg3WOeAYMl1y_virYISVHT1iwluxi_-VZM77GixfUsK6EvICphMEyWDqW8THSveyjqpVhp5-ODX3Jco59RuC_B75a6FAWIpcEYOG1ez1xXPdhnka_4eIwMa7ASKOGS3xaTLaNIkIjhTdFW6v1nAlrFuRMvez5DU64MF50aVtTzxZzLoWK0c2GIqel50SG7Kab-fDXBE1-AtfC1cDHkqps5HwsS1bO_jdsNc8usKkQIndzhinQ5UJCJekTItodhbGi0A%3D%3D)
---
We just announced what's next for Let Us Worship—REVIVE IN 25!!! Let Us Worship is heading across America to bring LIGHT into some of America's darkest cities; cities overrun with crime, drug-addiction, homelessness and hopelessness. We know the power of the Gospel is the answer, and we are called to be the messengers. _**WATCH THE GIVING TUESDAY MESSAGE ↓↓↓**_
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=249db20cce094cd696c27768ed9b63ad&_e=UObmW_65_LniOMe-wwf6BpsUvvmc3B9lsQuPHadzZ9h5WTTVonagF4Vt459uGs6DwiT96N6-Xgpm5l5IVGUxg3WOeAYMl1y_virYISVHT1iwluxi_-VZM77GixfUsK6EvICphMEyWDqW8THSveyjqpVhp5-ODX3Jco59RuC_B75a6FAWIpcEYOG1ez1xXPdhRHUSo4KOd2R5EUh2F-pNy7Nhnt2zeuph9t5llNnxD0rdlR6HWqWeT3fXcBepLO4rBZx5WxLcaOT9Ks-GE05YodigZyjWAFtBelsFEChyRKqlA7VrhsDn8NeDAa-PHQ-c056krqnHRhc8SJ3MoOLROQ%3D%3D)
---
Let Us Worship always has been and always will be an organic, grassroots ministry. We aren't bankrolled by a single benefactor, but rather an army of Believers, who like us, are desperate to see revival sweep across our land. That's why I am imploring you to help water the seeds of revival for 2025.
---
[**HELP FUND REVIVE IN 25 >>>**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=249db20cce094cd696c27768ed9b63ad&_e=UObmW_65_LniOMe-wwf6BpsUvvmc3B9lsQuPHadzZ9h5WTTVonagF4Vt459uGs6DwiT96N6-Xgpm5l5IVGUxg3WOeAYMl1y_virYISVHT1iwluxi_-VZM77GixfUsK6EvICphMEyWDqW8THSveyjqpVhp5-ODX3Jco59RuC_B75a6FAWIpcEYOG1ez1xXPdhmVgVnlxDbfd-b1BD-kfvZ8CpDq3sYGDTKJsyNegXUKbwTd7Add-6jlezTkoTEVnDoaZoP4W_LuAhYENHVJB-RvlH5VFI-PSxy3IN77_OBhf-46YcltblaBty5VXfbmu4IJMcYYo2kaInwzyaKVvhkw%3D%3D)
---
Our next mission, REVIVE IN 25, will bring the Gospel to the most downtrodden corners of America. Our mandate comes from Isaiah 61:4, "They will rebuild the ancient ruins and restore the places long devastated; they will renew the ruined cities that have been devastated for generations." Help us rebuild America's ruined cities with the life-changing power of the Gospel.
---
[**CONTRIBUTE TO GIVING TUESDAY >>> **](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=249db20cce094cd696c27768ed9b63ad&_e=UObmW_65_LniOMe-wwf6BpsUvvmc3B9lsQuPHadzZ9h5WTTVonagF4Vt459uGs6DwiT96N6-Xgpm5l5IVGUxg3WOeAYMl1y_virYISVHT1iwluxi_-VZM77GixfUsK6EvICphMEyWDqW8THSveyjqpVhp5-ODX3Jco59RuC_B75a6FAWIpcEYOG1ez1xXPdhLYq4WJXZj263nf5tL7gOEVDrXnqdmlxkVu4ROSg2-YyV8mALT_-AXbOAc4_MpXrQ3IKkfuHYyi_sSL1f6DSzov7_kpIkbr9hMMwKbLbQstHN9r5UrrEYnfEKbwGkX34fsURh6M5JVgdB-HH-c6Lj5g%3D%3D)

clean_body: See what God has in store for Let Us Worship| | | Dear Friend, Around the world today, non-profits are raising money for the specific charities they represent for Giving Tuesday. Let Us Worship is a Kingdom-First ministry, and we seek to see revival sweep across America and the world. We see Giving Tuesday as Kingdom Tuesday! We just announced what's next for Let Us Worship—REVIVE IN 25!!! Let Us Worship is heading across America to bring LIGHT into some of America's darkest cities; cities overrun with crime, drug-addiction, homelessness and hopelessness. We know the power of the Gospel is the answer, and we are called to be the messengers. WATCH THE GIVING TUESDAY MESSAGE ↓↓↓ Let Us Worship always has been and always will be an organic, grassroots ministry. We aren't bankrolled by a single benefactor, but rather an army of Believers, who like us, are desperate to see revival sweep across our land. That's why I am imploring you to help water the seeds of revival for 2025. Our next mission, REVIVE IN 25, will bring the Gospel to the most downtrodden corners of America. Our mandate comes from Isaiah 61:4, "They will rebuild the ancient ruins and restore the places long devastated; they will renew the ruined cities that have been devastated for generations." Help us rebuild America's ruined cities with the life-changing power of the Gospel. Till all hear, Sean | | | INTERNATIONAL DONORS— DONATE Donate by Check or Money Order Sean Feucht Ministries310 Third Street NEWashington, DC 20002 Contributions are tax-deductible. | | Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Let Us Worship

It’s not often you get a whole day dedicated to trees like me.
**But today is even more special. You have the chance to help Save the Redwoods League save my home, Sonoma Coast Redwoods.[When you give, it will be TRIPLED-MATCHED, up to $100,000!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088089/515518904/471999250?contactdata=Rz/YgHcHhsxFvT4bMsSB+XF2BjzAGxKrNtg1JOwwGvksYwJPVKyemGMzPNe3+nZBXll+JvZmawHcUlkEcM11KG4PIG9HPl+gzJ2gPCpYVE2un4uSZgVWl8c+g7HFvj9c0of2ooRjtOvh9amSzJPDphz++G3Fskf6tupMidJfB74=&ms=GT24_EM5_Link2&sourceid=1166159&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg0NmE5MWYwLTliYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LV-WgfE7M-ejnCBTyg94pVW7MvdGOvKcel4byQ0Cqng=&emci=8004dc92-caac-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=846a91f0-9bb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=1504551)**

You’ve probably heard about the ancient redwoods here—some of them are nearly 1,000 years old!
Well, that’s not me. I’m just a tiny seedling. It’s wonderful to look up and aspire to grow up to be one of the next towering old-growth redwoods. This is what makes Sonoma Coast Redwoods so special!
The age diversity (from young to ancient) of this forest means it’s set to FLOURISH in the decades to come. Every ancient redwood started as a seedling like me... so, like them, I’ll keep growing and growing. [**That is, if friends like you can help the League protect Sonoma Coast Redwoods.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088091/515518906/471999250?contactdata=Rz/YgHcHhsxFvT4bMsSB+XF2BjzAGxKrNtg1JOwwGvksYwJPVKyemGMzPNe3+nZBXll+JvZmawHcUlkEcM11KG4PIG9HPl+gzJ2gPCpYVE2un4uSZgVWl8c+g7HFvj9c0of2ooRjtOvh9amSzJPDphz++G3Fskf6tupMidJfB74=&ms=GT24_EM5_Link4&sourceid=1166159&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg0NmE5MWYwLTliYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LV-WgfE7M-ejnCBTyg94pVW7MvdGOvKcel4byQ0Cqng=&emci=8004dc92-caac-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=846a91f0-9bb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=1504551)

Plenty of people want to build private estates along the coast. I can’t blame them—it’s beautiful here! But development would be disastrous for the seedlings, trees, and wildlife that call this place home. Not to mention, I’d love to be visited one day by forest lovers like you on an expanded trail extension, instead of a few landowners.
**The League wants to purchase and protect the Sonoma Coast Redwoods from harmful development—forever. But they need YOUR help to do it![Make a difference on Giving TREESday with your 3X matched gift!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088093/515518908/471999250?contactdata=Rz/YgHcHhsxFvT4bMsSB+XF2BjzAGxKrNtg1JOwwGvksYwJPVKyemGMzPNe3+nZBXll+JvZmawHcUlkEcM11KG4PIG9HPl+gzJ2gPCpYVE2un4uSZgVWl8c+g7HFvj9c0of2ooRjtOvh9amSzJPDphz++G3Fskf6tupMidJfB74=&ms=GT24_EM5_Link6&sourceid=1166159&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg0NmE5MWYwLTliYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=LV-WgfE7M-ejnCBTyg94pVW7MvdGOvKcel4byQ0Cqng=&emci=8004dc92-caac-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=846a91f0-9bb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=1504551)**

clean_body: 3X match ends today Peter, Today is my absolute favorite day of the year: ! It’s not often you get a whole day dedicated to trees like me. But today is even more special. You have the chance to help Save the Redwoods League save my home, Sonoma Coast Redwoods. You’ve probably heard about the ancient redwoods here—some of them are nearly 1,000 years old! Well, that’s not me. I’m just a tiny seedling. It’s wonderful to look up and aspire to grow up to be one of the next towering old-growth redwoods. This is what makes Sonoma Coast Redwoods so special! The age diversity (from young to ancient) of this forest means it’s set to FLOURISH in the decades to come. Every ancient redwood started as a seedling like me... so, like them, I’ll keep growing and growing. Plenty of people want to build private estates along the coast. I can’t blame them—it’s beautiful here! But development would be disastrous for the seedlings, trees, and wildlife that call this place home. Not to mention, I’d love to be visited one day by forest lovers like you on an expanded trail extension, instead of a few landowners. The League wants to purchase and protect the Sonoma Coast Redwoods from harmful development—forever. But they need YOUR help to do it Thank you for giving me a chance to grow. 🌲 Yours Truly, A Sonoma Coast Redwood Seedling | Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States tel:415-362-2352 | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- |

Check out the sneak peak of the front cover of the Owyhee art book. **[Are you ready to donate for climate action and be entered to win?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088200/515519466/-1596352716?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU1ZTk2YjNhLTljYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KHN_YgQTMRqt3o4A5MHToWdkySluyPvbkkxYnF_qhqU=&emci=d3a93444-06b1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=e5e96b3a-9cb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=2107230)** We’ve been fighting for Oregon’s clean air and water, public lands, wildlife, and climate for over 50 years. We never stopped being Oregon’s political arm for the environment through Donald Trump’s last presidency, ensuring that Oregon’s clean air and water standards remained at the level they were before Trump took office. With your help, we can do it all again, and even better. If you make your contribution now, together we can ensure Oregon is still a leader on climate, regardless of threats at the federal level. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088201/515519467/-1596352716?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU1ZTk2YjNhLTljYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KHN_YgQTMRqt3o4A5MHToWdkySluyPvbkkxYnF_qhqU=&emci=d3a93444-06b1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=e5e96b3a-9cb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=2107230)While Donald Trump and a Republican dominated Congress are in office, our work these next few years are made more imperative. Especially in 2025. We must start out strong so we can continue to lead on issues like environmental transportation, affordable clean energy, climate resiliency within communities, and for our sustaining essentials like clean air and water. There’s nothing we cannot accomplish without a strong member base. Raising enough member dollars is the first step to further building up our member base. **A gift of $50 is enough to provide us the resources to recruit 1 new volunteer, $100 allows us to recruit 2 new volunteers, and a gift of $500 equates to 10 new volunteers.** These are the faces of our fight, the ones that will go out and speak to elected officials during town halls and lobby days. They’ll help us get petition signatures and speak to voters. [**Will you make your gift of $100 today and help us recruit 2 volunteers? If you’re feeling extra generous, a contribution of $500 will enter you in the Owyhee swag package raffle twice!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088202/515519468/-1596352716?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU1ZTk2YjNhLTljYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KHN_YgQTMRqt3o4A5MHToWdkySluyPvbkkxYnF_qhqU=&emci=d3a93444-06b1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=e5e96b3a-9cb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=2107230) No matter what happens at the federal level, we are committed to making sure Oregon remains the green state that it is, and continues to be a leader across the country for climate action. We cannot do this without you - we’re strong together and together we can get the job done. **[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088203/515519469/-1596352716?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU1ZTk2YjNhLTljYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KHN_YgQTMRqt3o4A5MHToWdkySluyPvbkkxYnF_qhqU=&emci=d3a93444-06b1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=e5e96b3a-9cb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=2107230)** Your gift this Giving Tuesday will not only be doubled by a generous donor, but it is one that will continue to give back to the climate movement. Each contribution will compound over time, creating an immeasurable impact on our work. Not to mention, you’ll be entered to win an exclusive Owyhee swag package. Don’t wait. [**Make your mark in the climate movement this Giving Tuesday and have your gift doubled!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98088204/515519470/-1596352716?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImU1ZTk2YjNhLTljYjEtZWYxMS04OGNmLTYwNDViZGZlOGQyOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KHN_YgQTMRqt3o4A5MHToWdkySluyPvbkkxYnF_qhqU=&emci=d3a93444-06b1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=e5e96b3a-9cb1-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=2107230) With gratitude,
Sarah Kline

| | Friend, Today is Giving Tuesday, a day when Americans unite to support causes that make a difference. At Club for Growth, we’re on the front lines fighting to shrink the size and scope of government, unleash pro-growth policies, and hold every legislator accountable on policy—no matter their party. subject: Thank President Biden for his leadership >>>>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| **Folks, with Election Day behind us, we've reached the beginning of the end of President Biden's Presidency.**

So we’re sending a HUGE Thank you letter to Biden for all of his service to our country.

[**We need 20,000 good Democrats to sign it with us — read below and sign the letter »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85228?t=6&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u) [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85228?t=7&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u) | [20,000 GOOD DEMOCRATS NEEDED: PLEASE GIVE $3 TO INSTANTLY SIGN THIS LETTER BEFORE WE CLOSE IT AT END-OF-DAY TODAY »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85228?t=8&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u)
---
Dear President Biden,

I have one thing to say to you: Thank you.

You have accomplished so much, and saved our country from the nightmare that Donald Trump left behind.

I remember how excited I was when you won in 2020.

I have had your back since you became President, and I am so proud of everything you've done for working families in our country.

You capped prices on prescription drugs.

You protected good-paying union jobs.

You passed historic legislation addressing climate change.

You have done everything in your power to strengthen our Democracy.

I have zero doubt in my mind: _You will go down as a truly GREAT American President._

[**I'm signing this letter and sending a small donation of $3 with VPP now to show that I'm still fighting — for our country and for your legacy, Mr. President. It's more important than ever that Democrats stand united together.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85228?t=9&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u) | [20,000 GOOD DEMOCRATS NEEDED: PLEASE GIVE $3 TO INSTANTLY SIGN THIS LETTER BEFORE WE CLOSE IT AT END-OF-DAY TODAY »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85228?t=10&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u)
---
**_X A THANKFUL AMERICAN_**
-The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=18123%2E878179%2Ejm4L2u)

name: Leaders We Deserve
subject: Our movement is changing the game
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Your support will power this crucial work in 2025 and beyond.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20241203-1&t=1&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)
Peter, we’ve got a special request from David Hogg and Leaders We Deserve:
We’re sure you’re getting a lot of emails about Giving Tuesday – and for good reason. This is an important moment for organizations and causes that are relying on a surge of grassroots support to reach their goals and carry out their missions.
**Leaders We Deserve is no different** – and today is a prime opportunity for us to build our movement and elect the next generation of young progressive leaders to office, _but that’s only possible with generous support from people like you._
We count on your support because we don’t take corporate money and neither do our candidates. That stacks the odds against our candidates, but that's where you come in to show it’s possible not only to run but to win _AND_ get the funding needed to cross the finish line.
**[We’re racing to meet our $10,000 Giving Tuesday goal before midnight. Can you pitch in $5 or more to power this crucial work in 2025 and beyond?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241203-1?refcode=em20241203-1490&amount=5&refcode2=1490_587540_wpxgxM&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241203-1?amount=5&refcode=em20241203-1490&refcode2=1490_587540_wpxgxM&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)**
Despite the disaster that the presidential election turned out to be, this team made important progress by electing six young candidates who now serve in their state legislatures and even Congress! We’re changing the face of power one election at a time – and **together, we are laying the foundation for an even stronger future.**
Our work has always been about playing the long game, about building the bench of young progressives today who will be the leaders we need in power tomorrow.
**[Peter, please contribute $5 or whatever makes sense for you now. With your support, we can reach our $10,000 Giving Tuesday goal before midnight.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241203-1?refcode=em20241203-1490&amount=5&refcode2=1490_587540_wpxgxM&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)**
Our victories this year – and the many wins still to come – are thanks to support from people like you. Thank you for being part of this game-changing movement!
We appreciate your support,
Leaders We Deserve
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20241203-1&t=2&refcode2=1490_587540_wpxgxM&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=1490%2E587540%2EwpxgxM)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

| | Friend, Today is Giving Tuesday, a day when Americans unite to support causes that make a difference. At Club for Growth, we’re on the front lines fighting to shrink the size and scope of government, unleash pro-growth policies, and hold every legislator accountable on policy—no matter their party. We never back down from a fight because we know what’s at stake: _your freedom and prosperity._ When others cave to the pressure of Washington’s broken system, we double down on our mission to protect taxpayers and promote economic growth. **[This Giving Tuesday, I’m asking you to support us.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/Bp82UX5dQLxm3OiN3Z-RwQ~~/AARclAA~-kNPZxhFakRSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAAAA)****** Your donation fuels our efforts to advocate for policies that will ensure America’s best days are ahead of us.
